summary: Tested affordability assignment block feature. Backend API correctly returns 422 with unaffordable_employees array when plan is unaffordable. Frontend Plan Library page loads correctly with MEC/MV badges, Assign buttons disabled for MV-failing plans (Platinum PPO), and Assign Employees dialog opens for MV-passing plans (Gold HMO, Silver PPO). The affordability-error-block JSX is properly implemented in PlanLibraryPage.js (lines 958-1001) to display when 422 error is caught.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/enrollment/plans/{plan_id}/assign-employees returns 422 for Platinum PPO ($744/mo) when assigning Adam Murphy ($49,782 salary)
Backend: 422 response contains message, unaffordable_employees array, and plan_cost
Backend: unaffordable_employees array contains: employee_id, name, annual_salary, employee_monthly_cost, max_affordable_monthly, pct_of_income, threshold
Backend: Assignment succeeds (200) for Silver PPO ($100/mo) - affordable for Adam Murphy
Backend: Assignment succeeds (200) for Gold HMO ($115/mo) - affordable for Adam Murphy
Frontend: Plan Library page loads with 8 plan cards
Frontend: MEC/MV badges display correctly on plan cards
Frontend: Platinum PPO Assign button is DISABLED (MV fail - correct behavior)
Frontend: Gold HMO Assign button is enabled (MV pass)
Frontend: Silver PPO Assign button is enabled (MV pass)
Frontend: Assign Employees dialog opens with 60 employee rows
Frontend: Dialog contains search input, Select All button, Save Assignments button
critical_code_review_comments: Backend affordability check in enrollment_workflow.py lines 583-616 correctly implements ACA 9.96% threshold check before assignment
Frontend catches 422 error in handleSaveAssignments (line 280) and sets affordabilityError state
Frontend affordability-error-block (lines 958-1001) renders table with employee salary, cost, max affordable, and % of income
Platinum PPO Assign button correctly disabled due to MV fail (line 430-441) - the 422 backend check is a safety net
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing plans and employees.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Affordability assignment block feature fully tested. Key findings: (1) Backend POST /api/enrollment/plans/{plan_id}/assign-employees returns 422 with detailed unaffordable_employees array when plan cost exceeds 9.96% of employee salary. (2) Frontend Assign button is disabled for MV-failing plans (Platinum PPO), so the 422 error is primarily a backend safety net. (3) The affordability-error-block JSX (lines 958-1001) is properly implemented to display when 422 is caught. (4) Test data: Platinum PPO ($744/mo EE cost) triggers 422 for Adam Murphy ($49,782 salary, max affordable $413.19/mo). Silver PPO ($100/mo) and Gold HMO ($115/mo) succeed.

verified_feature_details.feature_description: Affordability assignment block - 422 error when plan is unaffordable for employee

backend_implementation.description: POST /api/enrollment/plans/{plan_id}/assign-employees checks affordability before assignment. Returns 422 with unaffordable_employees array if plan cost > 9.96% of employee salary.

frontend_implementation.error_handling: Line 280 - catches 422 and sets affordabilityError state
frontend_implementation.error_block_ui: Lines 958-1001 - renders affordability-error-block with employee table
frontend_implementation.assign_button_disabled: Lines 430-441 - Assign button disabled when mvFails
